TextENGNew: "...Sensei, it's time."
"Mmm."
One of Chiba's secretaries who also served as his personal guard called out to him, waking him from the stupor he was in while resting in his seat.
Glancing at his watch, it was just a few minutes before boarding time. He felt like resting a little longer but he didn't want to fall asleep again, so he lightly stretched and then stood up.
"Here you go, sensei."
He grabbed the paper cup full of water offered to him and began to take a drink. ...His eyebrows furrowed for a moment as he sipped it, but then he drank it all down.
(...How inconsiderate.)
Normally they'd bring him a citrus-flavored beverage after waking him up, instead of foul-smelling airport water.
This man was still young, but at this rate he'd never be a suitable replacement for Koyama or Tsukada.
(What an idiot...@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_chiba1004. And I was thinking of assigning him to some local constituency if he could just follow orders.)
Although, he couldn't regret that the tail had been permanently cut off.
Now that the Deputy Prime Minister seat was within reach, plenty of talented people were trying to get close to him. He just had to pick the most impressive among them...
"Did you inform Okuno of tonight's meeting location?"

"Yes.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_secA1004. The roads in that area are quite complex, so I faxed a map to his office showing the location.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_secA1005. And I instructed his secretary to destroy the map upon confirmation."
"That's good.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_chiba1007. I definitely can't afford to feed anything to the prosecutors right now.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_chiba1008. Be mindful of anyone on our tail."
"Yes, sir."
...It was only the next morning that news got out about the raid at the Kakiuchi airport SDF base.
He turned pale when he heard the news. But after carefully thinking it over, Chiba came to the conclusion that there was nothing there to prove his involvement.

He made it clear to his intermediary, Yamashiro, that he should keep silent if he was ever caught. And the domestic subsidiary of Lowell Inc responsible for shipping these drugs was never even told Akihiko Chiba's name.

No matter how much information Tsukada might reveal in the future, without any evidence it could all be downplayed as nonsense. ...The bigger problem was Hanada, but he should be "on the run" for a while so it shouldn't be an issue any time soon.

He'd already taken many precautionary measures with Okuno since then. At this point, he just had to work with the prosecutor's office and the upper echelon of the police to reach a suitable agreement, and then this would all be over...
(Things are going to be messy for a while... but it can't be helped.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_chiba1010. It hurts to lose the financial support of Lowell Inc, but it was bound to happen sooner or later.)
And providing clinical trial samples and data brought in a great deal of money this time around. Even without Placil, there are plenty of other ways to make money.
In particular, he had colleagues vouching that the endemic disease research being conducted in the remote Hinamizawa village had practical applications.

Regardless of what he thought of the late Koizumi, just because the research was being terminated didn't mean they'd have to seal away the data, too. Best case scenario, it could be as valuable as a chicken that lays golden eggs...
Chiba smiled to himself as he rose to his feet.
Then, he turned toward the VIP boarding zone at the gate... but that moment.
"...Excuse me.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_sorima1281. You're the former Minister of Health and Welfare, Akihiko Chiba, aren't you?"
"What do you want?"
Chiba turned toward the man arrogantly standing in front of him and gave him a quizzical look, as if to say "isn't that obvious?"

The guards who had been waiting behind Chiba quickly stepped out front and braced themselves... but this man and his three cohorts behind him continued gazing at them with unflinching, piercing glares.

"I'm Chief Public Prosecutor Sorimachi, from the special investigation department at the Tokyo District Prosecutor's Office.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_sorima1283. On the charges of bribery, breach of Pharmaceutical Affairs Law, and violation of the Foreign Trade Act, you are hereby under arrest."
Then, the man named Sorimachi pulled an arrest warrant from his inside pocket, but Chiba just looked at it and snorted.
This man... is trying to arrest me?
The most senior member of the current ruling party, who even served as a cabinet minister... and yet he wants to arrest me?
"...There must be some mistake here.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_chiba1014. The elections are coming up soon and I'm very busy. I have no time to waste entertaining you."
"Hahaha.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_sorima1285. ...You've got a lot of nerve, after all hell broke loose last week.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_sorima1286. I hear you've already been briefed extensively by the higher-ups."
"What do you mean?@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_chiba1016. I have no idea what you're talking about."

"It seems the department heads and people at their level are acting under the Chancellor's orders.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_sorima1288. Unfortunately, that has no bearing on field workers like us.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_sorima1289. All we care about is analyzing the evidence and investigating the truth."
"...Our friends should be searching your home and your office right about now.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_sorima1291. With the proper warrants... of course."
Cold sweat ran down Chiba's back as soon as he heard that.
If they obtained not just an arrest warrant but also search warrants... it meant that the issuing court must've been presented considerable evidence.
And what's more... he should've been informed about this investigation well in advance, but this was completely new to him.
What was the meaning of this?!
"...How like the prosecutors office to waste time on baseless charges.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_chiba1020. Are you prepared for me to file charges of my own in retaliation?"

"I'm sure you've got a good lawyer on your side.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_sorima1293. But... let me just say one thing.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_sorima1294. No matter how skilled an attorney may be, they can't overturn the truth."
"I realize you're the type to play innocent and pin the blame on other people... but can you really do the same when there's hard evidence pointing otherwise?"
"...For example, that gentleman with the blonde hair you've been having excursions with at "Golden Spring.""

"The rulebook has gone through some changes.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_sorima1298. As of yesterday... Public Security officially decided to reopen the investigation.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_sorima1299. In other words, they now see you as an enemy of a law-abiding nation."
"Now... all right then.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_sorima1301. Please come along quietly."
On that stern declaration from Sorimachi, two subordinates behind him pushed Chiba's guards back and stood on opposite sides of him.
Chiba kept quiet, biting at his exceedingly dry lip...
"Huff... Ah."
She gently walked up the stairs holding a bouquet under her arm.
...And there was another visitor already at her destination ahead.
The girl holding her hands together in front of the grave must've noticed her footsteps, because she suddenly opened her eyes and turned to look.
Then, she smiled when she recognized who it was... but after looking more closely, she let out a cry of shock and stood up.
"M...Minai-san?@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1251. You're wounded! What happened?!"
"...It's no big deal.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe3272. It was a traffic accident... or, well, something like that."
Tomoe scratched at her cheek in embarrassment, then slowly hobbled down the cobblestone path on her crutches.
She'd never been injured like this before, so she was still getting the hang of it... but, well, it should only take a few months to get through it.
Although, he drove her all the way out here and even offered to help her... but after thinking it over for a while, she reluctantly declined his assistance.
She preferred to talk to Nagisa in private, if possible.
"More importantly... what are you doing here?@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe3274. Today is a weekday, so shouldn't you be in school?"
"Yeah.@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1253. Just for today, I asked my Dad if it would be all right to take a day off.@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1254. ...Is that so bad?"
"Hehe... I didn't see anything.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe3276. But you might run into trouble if you meet any weird adults... so why don't I give you a lift to the train station?"
"Thank you very much.@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1256. I'd be happy to take you up on that."
Upon accepting Tomoe's favor, the girl... Rena Ryuugu bowed and gave her a smile.

Her cheeks looked more flushed than the last time they met, and she seemed to be doing fine. Honestly, Tomoe was a little worried when she first saw her face, but now she was relieved and smiling back at her.
"...I came today to deliver a report.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe3278. To tell her that it's all over... and to say thanks."
"Huh?@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1258. You mean..."
"The people behind that drug that tormented you... have finally been caught.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe3280. I don't think we'll see that medicine ever again."
"...That's great.@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1260. Umm, thanks.@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1261. Really, Minai-san, it's all thanks to you..."

"No, actually... I should be the one saying thanks.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe3282. I was only able to catch the culprit thanks to your help.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe3283. Please let me show you my gratitude once more."
"Ahahaha... I'm glad.@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1263. Really, I'm so glad I met you."
"Likewise.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe3285. Also..."
As she said that, Tomoe sat the flowers she brought into the flower stand, then turned toward the grave, lit a stick of incense, and clapped her hands together.
And then, after silently staring at the grave for a while, she poured her heart into her words.

"Nagisa-san was the one who brought you and I together...@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe3287. I'll never forget her.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe3288. I'll always cherish my memories of her... for as long as I live."
"And I plan to keep visiting her whenever I get a chance.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe3290. The trip should be a lot easier starting next month."
"Huh...? You mean...?"
"I'm being transferred.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe3292. I'll be teaching at the police academy in Ibaraki for a while..."
Tomoe turned back toward Rena and playfully shrugged her shoulders.
Technically, it wasn't a transfer so much as it was... a demotion.
...Although, Tomoe was expecting to be fired and even face criminal charges, so this was a surprisingly warm response from HR.
"Well, consider it a little time to cool your head.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1325. I'm sure I'll call you back sooner or later, expecting you to pay me back double for this."
She could still remember the bitter smile on Maeno's face as he handed her the reassignment notice.
As far as he was concerned, this was the best way to punish Tomoe for acting out of line... while at the same time rewarding her for her effort.
And...
Tomoe heard from Maeno and the others that it was thanks to none other than him that her punishment ended up so light...
"...Are you really leaving?"
Fujita hesitated for a while as he cleaned the Chief's office, before asking that. Yamaoki, on the other hand, didn't pause for a moment and responded "yeah."
(Well... I figured someone would be forced to resign over this, but still...)
...That raid certainly wasn't seen in a flattering light.

All investigators of the First and Second Divisions at Kakiuchi Station received a flat three-month salary reduction. Hayami and Yoshikawa of the Mobile Investigation Team were not only given three-month salary reductions, but also transferred to another jurisdiction.
But the reason they were able to get off so lightly was because all the section managers and above took responsibility for it.
And... the person who suffered the most damage was the man at the top, Yamaoki.
Fujita felt sorry for him when he thought about it, and couldn't help but get upset at the unreasonable situation... yet the man in the suit didn't look depressed, but calm and unconcerned.

"Director Yamaguchi was able to set up transfers for Yamamura-san and Okabe-san.@k@vS14/31/VT4Bb_fujita1251. And Doujima-san is apparently being assigned as the principal of the police academy.@k@vS14/31/VT4Bb_fujita1252. So, why are you the only one being forced to resign, Yamaoki-san?@k@vS14/31/VT4Bb_fujita1253. Everyone else was allowed to stay..."

"Of course, the same was true for me too.@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1324. But... this investigation was a huge incident, and was unmistakably illegal.@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1325. Someone has to take responsibility for it."

"But... in the end, it was settled without the names of Lowell Inc or Congressman Chiba reaching the public.@k@vS14/31/VT4Bb_fujita1255. They're calling it the work of a violent crime group, who tried to sneak into the SDF base to smuggle out supplies."
Foreign pharmaceutical companies were smuggling unlicensed drugs into Japan, and medical institutions were conducting clinical trials with them.
And a huge amount of bribery was backing that data gathering effort...
Publishing news of such a large-scale scandal would destroy the reputation of the medical industry and send the economy onto turmoil.

So NPA, DPO, and CIRO... the Cabinet Intelligence and Research Office working for the Prime Minister, all came to an agreement that they'd only pursue Chiba's bribery charges related to foreign-affiliated companies.

The Defense Agency and the Ministry of Health and Welfare couldn't say anything to that. If things went poorly, it could lead to suspicion of their involvement and risk opening up further investigation.

As a result... the official story of the Kakiuchi Airport incident became that local police went in to investigate these armed burglars, and after it turned into a gunfight the riot police were sent in to help.
Additionally, the accident involving the cargo plane was declared due to engine failure, and the media was largely keeping to that story...

"Indeed, it's just as you say.@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1327. However... the politicians would not be amused if they saw that we did all that damage and not one person on our side lost their head over it.@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1328. This should give them at least a little relief."
"Chief..."
"What are you so worried about?@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1330. I've been facing significant cuts, but I'm still looking at some solid retirement pay."

"Besides, I was planning to return to my hometown of Yamaguchi after retirement, and if I inherit the orchard farmstead my parents own, I should be able to live comfortably with my current savings.@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1332. A peaceful second life... It really doesn't sound so bad."
"...@k@vS14/31/VT4Bb_fujita1258.Yeah, that's true.@k@vS14/31/VT4Bb_fujita1259. Maybe my wife and I can come out and visit you some time?"
"Of course, you're always welcome.@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1334. I'll even give you a special discount on our mandarin oranges."
"...You're charging money?"
After finishing boxing everything up and sending Fujita back to work, Yamaoki left the Chief's office for a breather. There, he ran into Madoka with perfect timing.
Seeing Yamaoki not dressed in his usual uniform, Madoka did a double take.
As of next week... Yamaoki would be retiring from the position of Chief, so she'd no longer be able to run into him while strolling through the station.
"...@k@vS14/29/VT4Bb_madoka1219.Thinking about it in hindsight, you always looked best in a uniform, Kaoru-san."
"Ha ha ha.@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1337. Hey, this is a good opportunity to learn a new fashion style.@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1338. I'm looking forward to it."
Yamaoki smiled wide enough to show the whites of his teeth.
...After all, this man was very mature, and he was always an ally of these two sisters.

In the decades since he was first appointed as an officer, he'd likely grown quite attached to it... but he was careful not to show any of that sentimentality to Madoka, lest it make her feel bad about it.
He was a truly kind... and warm person.
The depth of Madoka's feelings had her on the verge of tears, but she held them back and gave him a mischievous smile.

"Then we'll have to go shopping together next time.@k@vS14/29/VT4Bb_madoka1221. I did always think your clothes were pretty plain, Kaoru-san, so maybe this is a perfect opportunity?@k@vS14/29/VT4Bb_madoka1222. ...Please leave some time available next time there's a holiday, all right."
"Yeah, sure thing.@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1340. ...I'm sorry, Madoka-kun.@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1341. I wanted to throw a fancy wedding for you as the chief's wife."
"Hehe, it's fine.@k@vS14/29/VT4Bb_madoka1224. In fact, I'd prefer a smaller ceremony with just the family.@k@vS14/29/VT4Bb_madoka1225. So, don't you worry about it at all."
Then, Madoka grabbed Yamaoki's hand.
It was heavily wrinkled, but it was big... and robust, like a father's.
Madoka whispered to herself that she was right to choose this man, then gently rubbed her stomach.
"Then, I'll be going now."
"Let's keep up the good work... Papa♪"
"Papa...?@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1343. Wait, you mean...?"
"Eeeeeeeeeek!!!!!!!"
That evening.
A strange old man was seen running up and down the precinct, screaming and shouting.
For the sake of his honor, let's not say who it was.
"...A teacher?@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1266. I hope this doesn't sound rude, but I think that really suits you."

"Thanks.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe3294. Another girl I'm friends with told me the same thing.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe3295. She said she'd even like to be my student, if possible.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe3296. ...Although, she told me she was aiming to become a caregiver, so I dunno why she'd enroll in a police academy."
"Ahahaha."
Rena let out an amused laugh, then nodded to herself as though she could understand how this girl felt.

This was a woman who poured her heart into helping others... with so much compassion that anger and grief sometimes took control of her. Rena was certain her students would love her, and she'd be the kind of teacher they could consult with to get help on all sorts of problems.
Rena felt the same way about her teacher back at her own school.
"...Minai-san.@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1269. Honestly... you may not believe this, but... I met Nagisa-chan."
"Last night,@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1271. Nagisa-chan was standing at my bedside, telling me "it's all right now."@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1272. And... that's why I decided to come here today."
...If this was a dream, she wished so hard not to wake up.
If she was a ghost, she desperately prayed that she wouldn't leave.
But, after telling her that... Nagisa disappeared from the dark room.
She vanished... as if she was absorbed into that darkness...
"Rena-san..."

"...@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1274.I was so glad she came out to see me.@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1275. Really, really glad.@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1276. But... I just wanted a little more time.@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1277. Because... I never got to thank her, or tell her I'm sorry..."

"It's all right.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe3300. I'm sure your feelings got through to Nagisa-san.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe3301. The fact that you and I were able to meet here today is proof of that, isn't it?"
"...Minai-san."
"Because... I also came here to say thanks.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe3303. It's twice now that Nagisa-chan saved my life.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe3304. The fact that I'm alive at all is thanks to her."
...After Hanada was taken to prison, Tomoe was busy handling the paperwork for the case last night.
Hayami of MIT came to Kakiuchi station to show her the results of the vehicle inspection she'd requested.

"...Princess, the reason you were able to escape is because the lock on the back door was broken.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_hayami1080. The latch was bent and a screw was missing.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_hayami1081. An amateur mechanic would have a hard time noticing the problem at a quick glance."
"The lock... was broken?@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe3306. Why on earth would that be?"

"...@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_hayami1083.Maybe this is just my imagination,@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_hayami1084. but according to Hanada's confession, that same car was borrowed from Osamu Tsukada when he kidnapped Nagisa Ozaki."
"...And she fell out of the car while it was on the road, and was knocked unconscious... then she had the misfortune of being instantly killed by a passing truck."
"Nagisa Ozaki was in a drug-induced slumber in the back seat at the time... but when she woke up, she apparently tried to get out of the moving vehicle..."
Nagisa repeatedly smashed her body against the locked door, trying to break through.
Hanada laughed at the futility of it and kept on driving... but apparently the child safety lock wasn't working properly, and the door swung open after about a dozen strikes.
And in that moment, Nagisa's body had enough remaining momentum to be thrown out of the car...
That was Hayami's best guess at how it played out.
"...Is that really possible?"

"Normally, I'd say it's impossible.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_hayami1088. But the door really did open and Nagisa Ozaki did fall out.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_hayami1089. The forensic results corroborate Hanada's testimony."

"Then, he apparently repaired the damage himself before returning the car to Tsukada... but he didn't notice the damage on the lock itself.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_hayami1091. ...That seems to be how it happened."
"...Hanada's confession was actually instigated by forensic investigators identifying flower seeds in the backseat.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_hayami1093. They were tucked away in the gap between the seats."

"Based on those findings, we checked with the owner of the car, Osamu Tsukada... who confirmed that Hanada asked him to lend him the car on the day of Nagisa Ozaki's death.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_hayami1095. After we confronted Hanada with that information, he finally gave in and broke his silence."
"I see..."

"This may be a rude thing to say, but I'm grateful to the late Nagisa Ozaki.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_hayami1097. We were expecting this interrogation to drag on for an eternity, but we were able to get it done right away thanks to the evidence she left behind.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_hayami1098. She really saved us."
"...Yeah."
"And she saved me, too..."
"Minai-san, behind you!@k@vS14/35/VT4Bb_nagisa1078. The rear door!!@k@vS14/35/VT4Bb_nagisa1079. Break it!!!"
...That's right.
So... Nagisa knew the back door of that car would come open.
And she appeared in front of Tomoe when her life was in peril to deliver that message...
(Then... No way...?)
Just remembering that... caused Tomoe to catch her breath.
At the Kakiuchi airport, right when she was barreling into the plane.
Immediately after the collision, Tomoe rolled out of the car like she'd been pushed from the side by some force.
Thinking back on it now... it felt like something was violently pushing on her left shoulder... something like a human hand.
If that was the case... no, it seemed silly and unbelievable... but she could only think of a single possibility.
"...Is that so?@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1280. Then... I'm certain it was Nagisa-chan.@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1281. Somehow, it feels right."
"Yeah... I feel the same way.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe3314. ...No, more like I want to believe it.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe3315. So, I came here to say thanks."
...She still hadn't told Rena the truth of Nagisa's death. And she wasn't planning to tell her in the future. It wouldn't do her any good.
Rena Ryuugu... had finally made peace with her past, and was trying to step forward.
So now, Tomoe just wanted to support her for all her effort... and pray that she had a happy future ahead.
But just then...
"...Reina.@k@vS14/35/VT4Bb_nagisa1081. Minai-san."
All of a sudden.
Just as a stiff breeze blew through... Tomoe and Rena both looked up at the same time, feeling like they'd just heard a voice they could never forget.
And there, at the point where they both looked, was...
"...@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1284.Minai-san... can you see this?"
"...Yeah.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe3318. Rena-san... you're seeing this too?"
"Yes...!@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1286. Nagisa-chan... Nagisa-chan is...!!"
...She couldn't say anything more than that...
She... she never imagined she'd ever see Nagisa again... and yet, she was standing right there.
And... and...!
"...@k@vS14/35/VT4Bb_nagisa1083.Reina, I'm sorry about so many things.@k@vS14/35/VT4Bb_nagisa1084. ...I should have spoken to you sooner."
"...No... that's... that's not true!!@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1288. I didn't... do anything... for you and everyone else...!!"
...It was an unbelievable sight.
But... if this was a dream, she didn't want to wake up.
If it was an illusion, she wanted it to go on forever.
With that feeling burning in her heart, Rena began spilling tears and she stretched her hand out toward the figure.
But... that distance was far beyond her reach.
She tried to run closer, but her legs wouldn't move.
Still, she desperately squeezed at her choking throat, desperately trying to weave together the words she wanted to convey.
"I'm sorry... Nagisa-chan, I'm so sorry...!!"
"If only I'd believed in you more...!@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1291. If I could've been honest with you and confessed my feelings...!@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1292. I'm sorry, I'm sorry, I'm sorry...!!"
"...@k@vS14/35/VT4Bb_nagisa1086.Don't cry anymore, Reina."
Along with that voice... a hand gently touched Rena's cheek.
And then...
"I'm... really glad I got to have you as a friend."
"M-me too...!!@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1295. Me too, Nagisa-chan... I was so happy to have you as a friend!@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1296. Really... really, I was so happy!!"
Hearing those words... Nagisa smiled happily while standing in front of Rena, then turned to Tomoe standing quietly beside her... and silently bowed.
And when she raised her head, her body was bathed in a dazzling beam of light... then, she vanished.
"...Thank you, Reina.@k@vS14/35/VT4Bb_nagisa1089. And thank you, Minai-san..."
With those parting words... she finally left the two of them behind...
"...@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe3320.Rena-san, here..."
Tomoe was standing stunned for a while after Nagisa disappeared, but she suddenly came back to her senses and pulled a small cloth bag from her pocket.
Then she crouched down in front of Rena who was sobbing by her feet, and gently held it out to her.
"...What is this?"

"They're flower seeds.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe3322. Something Nagisa-san had with her until the end.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe3323. I'm sure she was planning to give these to you when she met you.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe3324. ...So, I want you to have them."
Rena's throat quivered as she stared at the bag... but she timidly reached her hand out to accept it. Then, she opened the bag and tilted it to the side, and many small particles came rolling out.
...The shape of the seeds looked familiar.
"These... are Japanese bellflower seeds.@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1301. They were always Nagisa-chan's favorite..."
"...Yeah.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe3326. Rena-san, do you know what Japanese bellflowers symbolize?"
"They mean "everlasting love.""

"Hey, Nagisa-chan.@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1304. We'll always be best friends.@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1305. Even after we grow older and move apart... let's have a good relationship, where we can talk about anything."
"Yeah, yeah!@k@vS14/35/VT4Bb_nagisa1091. We'll always, always be friends... I promise!@k@vS14/35/VT4Bb_nagisa1092. Forever! No matter where we are...!"
...That was the promise they made so long ago.
But the person Rena shared that promise with... was no more.
...However.
She vowed to never, ever forget those memories of caring and being cared for... nor Nagisa's smiling face.
Rena passionately grasped the small bag... while swearing that from the bottom of her heart...
